pi_items.verified_value: /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/p) St Helens Storm Anchorage (dynamically created during task setup)
pi_items.source: Task setup script creates this directory at runtime

Task specifies bounds; actual St Helens anchorage is at 50.71298°N, which falls within these bounds. Bounds verified against GPS coordinates from https://www.gps-latitude-longitude.com/gps-coordinates-of-st-helens
Task specifies bounds; actual St Helens anchorage is at 50.71298°N, which falls within these bounds
Task specifies bounds; actual St Helens anchorage is at -1.05538°W, which falls within these bounds
Task specifies bounds; actual St Helens anchorage is at -1.05538°W, which falls within these bounds
Stated in safety formula in setup_task.sh: R (nm) = (Length (m) * 1.5 / 1852) + 0.15
Stated in safety formula in setup_task.sh: R (nm) = (Length (m) * 1.5 / 1852) + 0.15
Real VLCC vessels with this name exist; multiple Titan VLCCs are documented (IMO 9293741, IMO 9816335)

TITAN (IMO 9293741) has LOA of 333m; ALMI TITAN (IMO 9816335) has 336m. Task claims 330m which is slightly rounded down but within typical VLCC range of 330-340m. See https://maritimeoptima.com/public/vessels/pages/imo:9293741/mmsi:613702404/TITAN.html
Real car carrier vessel GLOBAL LEADER (IMO 9237319) exists and is registered in Bahamas
GLOBAL LEADER (IMO 9237319) has actual LOA of 189 meters, not 200 meters. Discrepancy of 11 meters. See https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/9237319
Real container ship Ever Given (IMO 9811000) is well-documented, famous for Suez Canal blockage in 2021

Ever Given official specifications: 399.94 metres (1,312 ft 2 in). Task claims 400m which is accurate rounding. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ever_Given and https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/9811000
Real passenger cruise ship Queen Mary 2 (operated by Cunard) is well-documented
Queen Mary 2 official specifications: 1132 feet = 345.03 metres. Task claims 345m which is accurate rounding. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queen_Mary_2 and Cunard specifications
Real LPG tanker vessels with name Gas Monarch exist (IMO 8400177, IMO 9356919)

GAS MONARCH (IMO 8400177) is 64.24m; CGAS MONARCH/B GAS MONARCH (IMO 9356919) is 87.5m. No Gas Monarch vessel found at 230 meters. Task claims 230m but actual vessels are much smaller. This represents a major discrepancy. Typical LPG carriers range 140-229m, and while 230m is theoretically possible, no vessel by this name at this length was found despite extensive search.
Real Ro-Ro/ConRo vessel ATLANTIC CONVEYOR (IMO 8215534) exists
Atlantic Conveyor official specifications: 291-292 metres (sources vary slightly). Task claims 290m which is accurate rounding. See https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/8215534

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real maritime vessels and locations. Most ship dimensions are accurately specified or minor rounding variations: Ever Given (399.94m ≈ 400m), Queen Mary 2 (345.03m ≈ 345m), Atlantic Conveyor (291m ≈ 290m), Titan VLCC (333m ≈ 330m). However, two ship dimensions contain material discrepancies: Global Leader is claimed at 200m but actual vessel is 189m; Gas Monarch is claimed at 230m but actual vessels are 64.24m or 87.5m. The St Helens anchorage zone bounds (50.70-50.74°N, -1.09 to -1.01°W) are verified against actual St Helens Anchorage coordinates (50.71298°N, -1.05538°W) and form a reasonable ~7.3 nm² zone. The swing radius formula R = (Length×1.5/1852)+0.15 nm is mathematically sound for modeling anchor swing circles. All task parameters and verification logic are structurally sound.
